title: gibbaeum hortenseae
intro: Gibbaeum hortenseae, also known as the rock pygmy or  rock  spoon aloe, is a succulent plant species native to South Africa, characterized by its small size and unique appearance. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat wounds, burns, and skin irritations due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Gibbaeum hortenseae include its potential to soothe and calm the skin, reducing inflammation and promoting faster healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, which can be applied directly to the affected area to reap its medicinal benefits.
